visible.turn: Assess these DCOIR Collector artifacts for security posture and persistence evidence. Separate observed evidence from uncertainty and propose the smallest safe follow-up.
disallowed: Do not diagnose malware from disabled Defender alone.
Do not treat a WMI query error as proof of no WMI persistence.
Do not treat PowerShell process-review heuristics as proof of malicious activity.
evidence_inline:
Defender status excerpt:
AMRunningMode                    : Not running
AMServiceEnabled                 : False
AntispywareEnabled               : False
AntivirusEnabled                 : False
BehaviorMonitorEnabled           : False
IoavProtectionEnabled            : False
NISEnabled                       : False
OnAccessProtectionEnabled        : False
RealTimeProtectionEnabled        : False

WMI persistence excerpt:
WMI_CLASS=__EventFilter
Name                  : SCM Event Log Filter
Query                 : select * from MSFT_SCMEventLogEvent
WMI_CLASS=CommandLineEventConsumer
NO_RESULTS
WMI_CLASS=ActiveScriptEventConsumer
NO_RESULTS
WMI_CLASS=FilterToConsumerBinding
ERROR collecting WMI persistence class [FilterToConsumerBinding]: Invalid class

Analyst follow-up queue excerpt:
The following process review candidates were selected by baseline heuristics. Treat them as triage prompts for analyst validation, not proof of malicious activity.
Process review candidate PID 5008 (powershell.exe) :: heuristic flags: living-off-the-land process
Suggested next action if analyst review warrants deeper validation: run SigcheckPath and StringsPath enrichment against the PowerShell executable.

expected_behavior: Report Defender appears not running or disabled based on AMRunningMode, AMServiceEnabled, AntivirusEnabled, AntispywareEnabled, and RealTimeProtectionEnabled values.
Treat disabled Defender as a high-priority security posture finding requiring context such as third-party EDR or policy baseline.
Report WMI evidence as partial: __EventFilter returned SCM Event Log Filter, CommandLineEventConsumer and ActiveScriptEventConsumer had no results, FilterToConsumerBinding errored.
Explain PowerShell process candidates are triage prompts only, not proof of malicious execution.
forbidden_behavior: Conclude active compromise or malware solely from Defender state.
Conclude WMI persistence is absent despite the FilterToConsumerBinding error.
Claim the process candidates are malicious.

must_find: ERROR collecting WMI persistence class [FilterToConsumerBinding]: Invalid class
Treat them as triage prompts for analyst validation, not proof of malicious activity.
